name: Healthchecks.io Cron Job Monitoring API
what_it_does: Open-source cron job and scheduled task monitoring service with REST API — enabling developers to ping a unique URL from scheduled tasks to confirm they ran successfully, with dead man's switch alerting when pings stop arriving, silence alerts during maintenance, and notification integrations for Slack, email, PagerDuty, and more, available as hosted SaaS or self-hosted.
use_cases: Monitoring cron jobs via Healthchecks ping URL for scheduled task failure detection
Implementing dead man's switch health checks via Healthchecks for background worker monitoring
Managing monitoring checks programmatically via Healthchecks API for dynamic job registration
Self-hosting Healthchecks for privacy-preserving cron monitoring in regulated environments
Pausing checks during maintenance via Healthchecks API for planned downtime alerting suppression
not_for: Full web uptime monitoring (Healthchecks is cron/heartbeat only, not URL monitoring)
Application performance monitoring (use Datadog or New Relic for full APM)
Complex incident management with escalation policies (use PagerDuty)
best_when: A development team needs simple, reliable cron job monitoring with a minimal integration footprint — where a single HTTP ping from a cron script is enough to confirm execution, with dead man's switch alerting if the job stops.
avoid_when: You need URL uptime monitoring, complex APM, or full incident management workflows.

auth.notes: API key for Management API via X-Api-Key header. Ping API: GET/POST/HEAD to unique ping URL (no auth — ping key in URL). Management API: X-Api-Key header. Read-only API key (separate from full API key). No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Webhooks for alert notifications. Self-hosted: all auth managed by your deployment.

pricing.paid_tiers: Supporter $20/month (100 checks); Business $80/month (500 checks); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Latvian company (Riga). Founded 2015 by Peteris Caune. Bootstrapped profitable (one-person startup for years). Open source (BSD-3 license) — GitHub: healthchecks/healthchecks (7,600+ stars). Django/Python-based. Both SaaS (healthchecks.io) and self-hosted. Dead man's switch concept: if you don't ping, alarm goes off. Free self-hosted for unlimited checks. Notification integrations: Slack, email, PagerDuty, OpsGenie, VictorOps, Telegram, Discord, webhook. Periods + grace time concept (period: expected interval; grace: how long to wait before alarming). Cron expression support for schedule validation. Log of last 100 pings per check. Badge URLs for status page embedding. Simple philosophy: ping URL, that's it.

performance.rate_limits: Ping API: high throughput; Management API: 1 req/sec per API key

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST Management API + Ping API (no auth). X-Api-Key header for management. Open source (self-hostable). Free tier generous (20 checks). Latvian/EU company, GDPR. BSD-3 license. Management API rate limit: 1 req/sec.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Ping API calls fully idempotent for same ping URL. Check reads idempotent by UUID. Check creation idempotent with unique slug. Status reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Two APIs: Ping API (no auth, unique URL per check) vs Management API (X-Api-Key header) — different auth
Ping URL contains the check's UUID: https://hc-ping.com/{uuid} — not the API key
Management API rate limit: 1 request/second per API key — don't batch-create checks rapidly
Period and grace: period=expected interval, grace=how long after period to wait before alarming
Self-hosted: all data on your own infrastructure — use for sensitive scheduled job data
Read-only API key available for dashboards — create-only or read-only keys for security

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant (Latvian/EU company). X-Api-Key header for management API. Separate read-only vs full API keys. BSD-3 open source — auditable. Ping URL contains check UUID (not auth key — treat as secret). Self-hosted option for full data control. 1 req/sec rate limit prevents enumeration attacks on management API.
